narration: After fast and slow meet, we know we are standing inside the loop.
callout: The meeting point is a safe place to start measuring.
decision: Now walk around the cycle and count the steps.

narration: Move one pointer around the loop until it returns to the meeting node.
callout: Each step adds 1 to the cycle count.
decision: One full trip around the loop gives the length.

predictionPrompt.prompt: Why can we start counting after the meeting point?

choices.label: Because the meeting point is already inside the loop
choices.feedback: Correct. One full walk from there measures the loop.

choices.label: Because it is always the head
choices.feedback: Not this one. The meeting point is usually not the head.

narration: When the pointer returns to the same loop node, the count is the cycle length.
callout: In this example, one full trip takes 3 steps.

reflectionPrompt.prompt: Why does one full trip around the loop measure the cycle length exactly?
